channel.title: The Grant Desk - EU, next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: EU, next 30 days.

item.title: Feminist Gaming Fellowship: Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Feminist Gaming Project

Location: Remote across the Netherlands, Türkiye, Germany, and the UK

Award: EUR 1,000 honorarium per fellow (paid half at the start, half on completion)

Duration: Three months, August to October 2026; minimum ~6 hours per week (~75 hours total)

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

The Feminist Gaming Project is opening applications for its first Fellowship cohort: five fellows from different creative and research fields spending three months developing 3D assets, tools, and documentation for its Open Access Asset Library (OAL), a free, open repository of inclusive 3D content created by, with, and for intersectionally feminist and diverse communities. Where mainstream marketplaces default to hyper-athletic, idealized, or hyper-sexualized characters, the OAL offers average bodies, real postures, everyday objects, and the kinds of lives most games leave out (pregnancy and postpartum, care work, ageing, disability, domestic labor). This first cohort is dedicated to 3D content. Supported by the Creative Industries Fund NL and working across the Netherlands, Türkiye, Germany, and the UK, the project challenges exclusionary norms in how games are made. The fellowship offers a EUR 1,000 honorarium per fellow over three months (half at start, half on completion); mentorship from the core team and advisors in feminist design, game studies, 3D production, and digital culture; a peer community across NL, TR, DE, and the UK with biweekly meetings; visibility through a travelling exhibition program, publications, and public events; and authorship credited in the OAL under its license terms. Five roles are recruited (one fellow each): 3D Character Artist for inclusive base meshes; 3D Character Artist for pregnancy and postpartum assets; 3D Animator for neutral and everyday movement; 3D Modeller for domestic and care-labor environments; and Researcher/Writer for documentation and the representation framework; applicants may rank up to two. Open to 3D artists, animators, modellers, designers, researchers, and writers committed to inclusive and intersectional representation in games; prior game-industry experience is not required, and emerging creators, underrepresented practitioners, and researchers at the intersection of games, gender, and culture are especially encouraged, particularly those based in or connected to NL, TR, DE, or the UK. To apply, email feministgamingproject@gmail.com with a short statement (max ~500 words), a portfolio or work samples (a link is fine), and confirmation of availability for the three-month period; name the role(s) in the subject line or statement. The project explicitly asks applicants to write statements in their own voice rather than using AI-generated text. Indicative timeline: applications close 12 July 2026; selection and notification late July; fellowship period August to October 2026; showcase and publication November to December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CIRCLE Fiction Orbit 3rd Edition
item.description:
Organisation: CIRCLE Training Initiative

Location: Herceg Novi, Montenegro

Award: Accommodation and meals provided; in-depth script consultations, legal and production strategy guidance, career development mentorship, and psychological support. Travel not covered. Participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person applies. At least one 50 percent scholarship available annually for participants from lower-income countries, those without national funding, or with demonstrated financial need. EUR 2,000 participation fee (accommodation and meals included; travel not covered); 50 percent scholarship available for demonstrated need

Duration: One week

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

CIRCLE Fiction Orbit is a one-week retreat in Herceg Novi, Montenegro for up to six fiction feature film projects led by teams of women and gender-expansive filmmakers developing their second, third, or fourth narrative feature. Participants work with leading experts on script development, legal and production strategy, career positioning, and receive psychological support throughout the creative process. Applicants must identify as a woman or gender-expansive filmmaker, work as a screenwriter, director, or producer, have completed at least one feature, and submit a project with a producer attached. Accommodation and meals are provided, but travel is not covered, and a participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person applies. At least one 50 percent scholarship is available annually for those with demonstrated financial need. The third edition runs October 25 to November 1, 2026, with applications due July 10, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CIRCLE Expanded Orbit 1st Edition
item.description:
Organisation: CIRCLE Training Initiative (with Onassis ONX)

Location: Athens, Greece

Award: No cash stipend. Provides individual mentorship from world-leading experts, group sessions, co-creation workshops, structured feedback, and access to an international professional network across film, art, design, and technology. Participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person (EUR 4,000 per team of two), with scholarships covering up to 50 percent available for those with financial need. EUR 2,000 participation fee per person (EUR 4,000 per team of two); up to 50 percent scholarships available

Duration: 6 days (in-person training)

Deadline: 02 Jul 2026

CIRCLE Expanded Orbit is the first edition of an international training programme dedicated to immersive storytelling and XR-based practices, run by the CIRCLE Training Initiative in partnership with Onassis ONX. It supports women and gender-expansive filmmakers, artists, XR creators, and multidisciplinary storytellers working across XR, MR, VR, AR, AI, and game-based narratives, with no prior XR experience required. Selected individuals or teams of up to two receive individual mentorship, group sessions, co-creation workshops, and structured feedback, plus access to an international network of professionals. The in-person training takes place from November 21 to 26, 2026, in Athens, Greece. Applicants should be developing a project with a strong artistic vision and clear interest in immersive formats, ideally with a producer attached and partial budget secured. A participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person applies, with scholarships of up to 50 percent available based on financial need.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artist Residency Autumn 2026 (Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies, Palermo)
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies

Location: Altarello di Baida district, Palermo, Sicily, Italy. On-site 62-day residency, 12 October - 12 December 2026. Open to all nationalities, but applicants must be officially residing in a Creative Europe country (excluding Italy).

Award: Total allowance of EUR 1,860 (before tax) per artist, plus a travel budget of EUR 400 per artist (for distances under 4,000 km) with a EUR 400 green-mobility top-up, plus a production budget. Also provided: on-site accommodation in a fully furnished independent house (private bedroom; shared kitchen, living room, two bathrooms; outdoor studio space 100m from the working site), access to recycled/salvaged materials through the Centre's partnerships, a bicycle per resident, and a final public event.

Duration: 62-day residency, 12 October - 12 December 2026, structured in five phases (orientation, observation/fieldwork, proposal and ecological feasibility, creation, and public restitution).

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

The Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies invites applications from two artists for a 62-day residency (12 October - 12 December 2026) at its site in the Altarello di Baida district of Palermo, Sicily. The residency is structured around a 1,500 sqm garden at the threshold between the city's urban expansion and the historic rural landscape of the surrounding hills; the site, degraded through previous use (soil compaction and significant biodiversity loss), functions as both studio and subject, and artists participate in an ongoing process of ecological transformation, producing interventions that emerge from extended observation and fieldwork. Applications are welcome across all mediums and disciplines (installation, sound, performance, video, food art, participatory practice, and beyond); research-based and interdisciplinary practices are especially encouraged. The Centre works at the intersection of art, science, and (agro)ecological practices, with a focus on multispecies coexistence and climate change in the Mediterranean, and has been based in Palermo since 2021. PROGRAMME: five phases (orientation with small trips and gatherings; observation with independent fieldwork and consultations with artists, curators, scientists, and anthropologists; proposal and ecological feasibility developed with mentors; creation; and public restitution with an open event, a workshop, and a public discussion). Each artist develops an independent site-specific intervention; the two residents share the site, living space, and process, with exchange encouraged but not prescribed. Artist duos and artists who want to collaborate are encouraged to apply (you can apply as an individual for one position, as a duo for both, or as two artists collaborating for two positions). Mentorship by co-founders/artistic directors Fabio Aranzulla and Luca Cinquemani (weekly site visits, studio discussions, work-in-progress presentations); logistics managed by Francesca Farris. WHO: artists whose work engages with ecology, multispecies relations, or the more-than-human, who can move between observation and making and are interested in dialogue with scientists, land workers, and local communities. Priority is given to artists from working-class and less-privileged backgrounds, those who self-identify as part of marginalised communities, and candidates reflecting diversity in gender, age, geographical background, and ability; green travel is prioritised as a condition of participation where feasible. Applicants should be open to living and working in a neighbourhood on the margins of Palermo's periphery. Funded by Culture Moves Europe (Creative Europe programme of the European Union). APPLICATION: first phase via the online form (opened 10 June); shortlisted artists contacted before the end of July; second phase is video calls with shortlisted candidates; applications accepted in English and Italian. Contact: contact.cmrfe@gmail.com. Deadline 5 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AY-ROOP / Le Milieu: Reuse - (Re)making for Circus Residency 2026 (Rennes Métropole, France)
item.description:
Organisation: AY-ROOP

Location: Le Milieu, Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole, France. Applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe country other than France and the French overseas territories (DROM-COM).

Award: Funded through Culture Moves Europe: EUR 900 mobility allowance per person plus flat-rate travel coverage; supplements for green mobility, visa fees, dependent children under 18, OCT/OR residents, and disability/accessibility needs (including possible full coverage of a sign-language interpreter). On-site single-room accommodation, lunches at Le Milieu canteen (Tuesday to Friday), shared kitchen, and technical support provided. No application fee.

Duration: 21-day residency, 28 November to 18 December 2026.

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

AY-ROOP is launching an open call to select three circus artists and/or circus set designers/builders for a 21-day Culture Moves Europe research residency at Le Milieu (Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole) from 28 November to 18 December 2026. The residency is a research laboratory on reuse in contemporary circus: how everyday, locally available materials (paper/cardboard, plastics, lightweight metal, textiles, reclaimed wood, bicycle parts, etc.) can be used to create scenographic elements, sets, props and small apparatuses for juggling, acrobatics and balancing. The core principle is that objects are recreated locally from resources available in each territory rather than transported. AY-ROOP provides an equipped construction workshop, a rehearsal stage and circus tent, a guide to local reuse-sector contacts, and a full residency environment (accommodation, shared kitchen, working facilities); two mentors (a reuse specialist and a performing-arts professional) support the artists. PROFILES: circus artists interested in construction/scenography/DIY making, and builders/set designers working in circus; individual or group applications (max 3 people per group); emerging or established, any background. Accessible to artists with disabilities and accessibility needs. Working language English. No aerial apparatus or rigging: all work is ground-based. ELIGIBILITY: aged 18+ (no upper limit); legally resident in a Creative Europe country other than France and DROM-COM; available for the full residency. APPLY with a single PDF (1-page bio/CV, 5-page portfolio or video links, 1-page statement of intent, proof of residence). DEADLINE: 3 July 2026. Results announced 13 July 2026. Check the linked call PDF for full details.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: European Commission: Safeguarding Europe's Born-Digital Heritage (PPPA-2026-BORN-DIGITAL)
item.description:
Organisation: European Commission (Pilot Projects and Preparatory Actions, Culture strand)

Location: European Union. Consortium of at least 5 independent entities from 5 different EU member states required.

Award: Up to €1,985,000, covering up to 85% of eligible project costs (15% co-financing required). No application fee.

Duration: Project duration to be defined in the proposal; covers a scoping and mapping phase across EU member states.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

The European Commission is offering a single grant of up to €1,985,000 (85% co-financing) for a project focused on safeguarding born-digital cultural heritage across EU member states. Scope includes: assessing the preservation state of born-digital art, web archives, video games, and digital content created outside cultural institutions; mapping EU and national legal frameworks; identifying non-legislative barriers (technical, financial, organisational, cultural); and providing guidance on creating preservation copies of licensed or legally accessible materials. This is a scoping and advocacy project, not a digitisation grant. ELIGIBILITY: the application must be led by a consortium of at least 5 independent entities from 5 different EU countries, which rules out solo applicants and single-country organisations. Grant reference: PPPA-2026-BORN-DIGITAL. Deadline: 16 July 2026. Verify the current deadline at ec.europa.eu before applying (one secondary source listed 1 June 2026; the primary call-fiche PDF and the Cyprus government funding portal both confirm 16 July 2026).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Simone Veil Prize for European Jewish Cultural Heritage 2026
item.description:
Organisation: European Commission, Directorate-General for Education, Youth, Sport and Culture

Location: EU Member States and third countries associated to the Creative Europe programme

Award: €10,000 per Grand Prix winner (five winners, one per category); up to 25 finalists recognized. No application fee.

Duration: For projects completed between January 2023 and January 2026

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Simone Veil Prize, administered by the European Commission, recognises outstanding projects preserving and promoting European Jewish cultural heritage. It is awarded across five categories: Shared Histories, Heritage for Dialogue, Living Heritage, Cross-border Cooperation and Shared Narratives, and Next Generation/Youth Engagement and Education. AWARD: €10,000 per Grand Prix winner (one per category, five in total); up to 25 finalists receive recognition. ELIGIBILITY: legal or natural persons (individuals or organisations) registered in EU Member States or third countries associated to the Creative Europe programme; projects must have been completed between January 2023 and January 2026. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026 at 23:59 CET. APPLY by submitting the completed application form by email to applications@simoneveilprize.eu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TBA21-Academy: Tentacular Fellowship 2026-2027 (Ocean Space, Venice)
item.description:
Organisation: TBA21-Academy, with NICHE Centre for Environmental Humanities and the UNESCO Chair on Water, Heritage and Sustainable Development at Ca' Foscari University

Location: Ocean Space and Ca' Foscari University, Venice, Italy; situated work in and with the communities of the Lagoon of Venice.

Award: Total fellowship support EUR 26,000. Phase 1 (incubation): EUR 3,000 grant for independent research and initiative development. Phase 2 (activation): EUR 8,000 grant plus a EUR 15,000 activation budget. No application fee.

Duration: 15 months, September 2026 to November 2027

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

TBA21-Academy, in collaboration with NICHE Centre for Environmental Humanities and the UNESCO Chair on Water, Heritage and Sustainable Development at Ca' Foscari University, is launching the first edition of the Tentacular Fellowship, an initiative-building fellowship based at Ocean Space and at Ca' Foscari in Venice. Conceived as a long-term platform for artistic research and ecological engagement, Ocean Space works to develop new forms of action capable of moving beyond awareness-raising to produce real, tangible territorial effects through situated collaboration with the communities, practitioners, researchers and civic actors already working within the Lagoon of Venice. The fellowship invites a practitioner or collective to initiate, consolidate or expand a situated initiative in the Lagoon through the creation of a Tentacular Unit: a collaborative, interdisciplinary platform able to gather artists, students, researchers, institutions, local communities and other actors around a specific socio-environmental challenge. Through situated research, alliance-building and public-facing formats, the fellow helps build the social, institutional and ecological conditions for the initiative to take root and grow over time. STRUCTURE: the two-year fellowship runs in two phases. In Phase 1 (incubation) the fellow is awarded a EUR 3,000 grant to support independent research and initiative development. In Phase 2 (activation) the fellow is awarded a EUR 8,000 grant plus a EUR 15,000 activation budget, for total fellowship support of EUR 26,000. Details on the application process, evaluation criteria and frequently asked questions are in the full open-call document. Application deadline: 5 July 2026, 11:59pm CET. Apply via the online form linked from the open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Iméra: 'This Is Not Another Fellowship' – Postdoctoral Residency 2026-27 (Aix-Marseille)
item.description:
Organisation: Iméra – Institute for Advanced Study at Aix Marseille Université

Location: Aix-Marseille, France (campus residency)

Award: Free accommodation in campus apartment; travel expenses covered; office in co-working space. No cash stipend specified. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months: either November 2, 2026 – January 26, 2027, or March 1, 2027 – May 28, 2027.

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

Iméra's 'This Is Not Another Fellowship' is a 3-month postdoctoral residency at the Institute for Advanced Study at Aix Marseille Université, offered in two semester options: November 2, 2026 – January 26, 2027, or March 1, 2027 – May 28, 2027. PROVIDED: free accommodation in one of fourteen campus apartments; travel expenses covered; desk in shared open co-working space; participation in the weekly Community Building Seminar (mandatory 3-hour Thursday sessions); access to interdisciplinary scholarly networks. ELIGIBILITY: under 35 years old by May 28, 2027; currently holding a postdoctoral fellowship contract at a university; have not lived in France for more than 12 months in the prior 3 years; if requiring a visa, must demonstrate sufficient funds. Research must align with one of four Iméra programs: Arts & Sciences: Indisciplined Knowledge; Interdisciplinary Dialogues; Mediterranean; or Necessary Utopias. All disciplines welcome. DEADLINE: 3 July 2026 at 1pm (CET+2). Apply at https://www.imera.fr/en/this-is-not-another-fellowship-application-2026-27/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: EIC Accelerator 2026 (European Innovation Council)
item.description:
Organisation: European Innovation Council (European Commission)

Location: EU Member States and Horizon Europe Associated Countries. Single-company applicants; third-country applicants must relocate before the full-application stage.

Award: Grant up to EUR 2.5 million (non-dilutive lump sum) for innovation activities, plus optional equity investment of EUR 1-10 million via the EIC Fund. Options: grant-only, blended finance, or investment-only. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; supports development and scale-up at TRL 5/6 to 8.

Deadline: 08 Jul 2026

The EIC Accelerator funds high-risk, high-impact deep-tech SMEs and start-ups to develop and scale breakthrough innovations. It offers a grant of up to EUR 2.5 million (a non-dilutive lump sum) for innovation activities, and can combine this with an equity investment of EUR 1-10 million through the EIC Fund (blended finance), or provide investment only. The Step 1 short application can be submitted at any time; full (Step 2) applications are evaluated at fixed cut-offs. The next 2026 cut-offs are 8 July, 2 September and 4 November 2026 (all 17:00 Brussels time). Eligible applicants are for-profit SMEs and start-ups (fewer than 250 employees, turnover up to EUR 50M or balance sheet up to EUR 43M) established in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe Associated Country; small mid-caps up to 499 employees are eligible for investment-only support. Apply via the EU Funding and Tenders portal (topic HORIZON-EIC-2026-ACCELERATOR-01).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Circolo del Design: Earthrise 26 Residency for Young Designers 2026 (Turin)
item.description:
Organisation: Circolo del Design

Location: Turin, Italy; in-person residency.

Award: Designer's fee of EUR 1,500 (VAT included); travel and accommodation covered for both phases of the residency; a public transport pass; up to EUR 3,000 (VAT included) for project production; a dedicated 30 sqm workspace within Circolo del Design; plus networking, mentorship from interdisciplinary experts, and technical, logistical and organisational support. No application fee.

Duration: Residency 5 October to 3 December 2026

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

Earthrise 26 (Design for a Living Planet), the annual cultural initiative by Circolo del Design, will unfold around clay and earthenware, approached not as functional objects but as narrative, spatial and speculative matter. One of the projects featured in the exhibition will be developed through the Earthrise 26 Residency (5 October to 3 December 2026), a unique opportunity for an under-35 international designer to create and experiment within an interdisciplinary context. Under the mentorship of Designregio Kortrijk and BASE Milano among others, the selected resident will collaborate with local designers, artisans and interdisciplinary experts in Turin to produce an original body of work exhibited as part of the Earthrise 26 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: under 35; a demonstrated international practice through research, collaborations, cultural exchange or mobility within Europe; well-structured project planning with clear goals; thematic relevance aligned with the objectives of Earthrise 2026; and an experimental and/or innovative approach. WHAT IS OFFERED: a designer's fee of EUR 1,500 (VAT included), travel and accommodation for both phases, a public transport pass, up to EUR 3,000 (VAT included) for project production, a dedicated 30 sqm workspace, and mentorship and technical/logistical/organisational support. Organised by Circolo del Design (circolodeldesign.it; contact programma@circolodeldesign.it). Deadline: 3 July 2026; results announced 20 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: (Perma)culture Open Call 2026 (Ceske Budejovice 2028 European Capital of Culture)
item.description:
Organisation: Ceske Budejovice - European Capital of Culture 2028

Location: Projects implemented in Ceske Budejovice / South Bohemian Region, Czech Republic

Award: Large projects: support up to CZK 4,000,000 (approx. EUR 160,000), min budget CZK 2,200,000, max 50% of total costs. Small projects: support up to CZK 1,500,000 (approx. EUR 60,000), min budget CZK 750,000, max 70% of total costs. Each project must include at least one international partner and at least one South Bohemian regional partner. Budget covers two years of preparation and implementation (2027 + 2028). No application fee.

Duration: Artistic/cultural output delivered for the public in 2028; budget covers preparation and implementation across 2027-2028

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

Second open call from Ceske Budejovice - European Capital of Culture 2028, expanding the ECoC 2028 programme. It funds cultural projects with an artistic or cultural output for the public in 2028, implemented in Ceske Budejovice or the South Bohemian Region, built on regional, national and international cooperation. The 2026 call theme is (Perma)culture as a content framework (care for the Earth, people and the future) reflected through a project's process, content and/or impact, rather than as a literal agriculture/ecology theme. All cultural and artistic fields are eligible, with extra evaluation points for: contemporary and conceptual fine art (site-specific/public-space installations, participatory and intermedia projects); audiovisual, multimedia and digital arts, immersive content, video game development and film (immersive AV installations, interactive digital projects, videomapping, VR/AR/XR, art/educational games, digital art working with data, AI or game principles); architecture; contemporary design and fashion; contemporary music and sound art; and photography. ELIGIBILITY: the 2026 call is open to legal entities (organisations) with no territorial restriction (South Bohemia, Czech Republic, EU and worldwide). Individuals cannot apply but can join project teams. APPLICATION: submit the application and mandatory attachments by email to opencall@budejovice2028.cz (subject '(Perma)culture Open Call 2026 + [Applicant Name]') and via the registration form, plus submission via data box per the call conditions. Deadline 10 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ettijahat: Zad Mobility for Arab Artists in Europe (1 July 2026 Cut-Off)
item.description:
Organisation: Ettijahat - Independent Culture

Location: Mobility within Europe (current country of residence and across Europe) for artists from the Arab region now residing in Europe

Award: Financial contribution covering: travel and accommodation (transportation, internal transport, daily expenses, travel insurance including special-needs/health-related costs); travel costs for children and partners plus childcare costs if parents cannot travel without them; transportation and shipment of artworks and production materials; translation of artworks into multiple languages and simultaneous translation during live audience meetings. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based mobility (variable)

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

Ettijahat's Zad programme is a support framework promoting mobility and communication for artists from the Arab region residing in Europe, designed to develop their artistic and professional path in collaboration with peers and across artistic spaces and platforms, while helping them reach diverse audiences and contribute to public life in their cities/countries of residence. SUPPORTED ACTIVITIES (examples): presenting theatrical works and live artistic performances; concerts at music festivals; streaming films in cities/places where they have not previously been shown; organising or joining literary readings; organising and relocating art exhibitions; sharing the outcomes of artist residencies. DISCIPLINES: all artistic and literary specialisations; open to artists of all ages. ELIGIBILITY: individual or group artists from the Arab region regardless of ethnic background who have relocated to Europe since 2015. PRIORITY for artists who moved from Syria, Palestine, Lebanon, Iraq, Jordan, Egypt, Yemen, Sudan, and Libya. The programme encourages environmental and economic solutions when planning travel. DEADLINE: this card is for the 1 July 2026 cut-off (other cut-offs each have their own card). APPLY via the Ettijahat Zad page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Aerowaves Twenty27: Call for Emerging European Choreographers
item.description:
Organisation: Aerowaves (Spring Forward festival hosted in Limerick, Ireland)

Location: Festival in Limerick, Ireland (14-17 April 2027) plus partner venues across Europe; applicants must be resident in geographical Europe

Award: For presentation at Spring Forward: a fee plus expenses including travel within Europe, hotel and a per diem/hospitality for each team member. Around 100 partner performance opportunities are guaranteed each year and supported by Aerowaves. Selected applicants additionally receive a year of Aerowaves promotion (artist profile, images, video, press coverage). No application fee.

Duration: One year of Aerowaves promotion and partner-network presentations; presentation at Spring Forward 14-17 April 2027

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Aerowaves selects 20 emerging choreographers based in geographical Europe each year (the Twenty27 cohort) to have their work presented at the Spring Forward festival (14-17 April 2027 in Limerick, Ireland) and at many of Aerowaves' partners around Europe; Aerowaves promotes the selected artists for a year, creating performance opportunities with partners. EVERY APPLICANT (selected or not) has the chance to be programmed by Aerowaves network partners; around 100 performance opportunities per year are supported by Aerowaves. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be resident in geographical Europe; due to the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Aerowaves partners do NOT accept applications of works made in Russia or Belarus. ONE work per applicant per year. The work must have been produced in geographical Europe; must be 15-40 minutes; must be finished and have been presented to the public (a studio sharing counts; work-in-progress or rehearsal footage does NOT). The work should be easily included in a double or triple bill with simple technical requirements; works created for public/non-conventional spaces and outdoors are welcome. WHAT SELECTED ARTISTS GET: presentation fee at Spring Forward plus travel (within Europe), hotel and per diem/hospitality for each team member; a year of Aerowaves website promotion. DEADLINE: 15 July 2026. APPLY via the Aerowaves how-to-apply page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Faber Andorra Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Faberllull

Location: La Massana, Andorra

Award: EUR 60 daily meal allowance plus fully equipped apartment (free) and 30 sqm visual/performing arts workshop. Travel and medical insurance NOT covered. No application fee.

Duration: 2-6 weeks, between February and November 2027

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

Individual interdisciplinary residency in La Massana, Andorra, in a natural mountain setting. ELIGIBILITY: open worldwide to any professional from the arts, sciences and humanities with a project requiring focused working time; visual and audiovisual arts, crafts, photography, dramaturgy, performing arts, cinema, history, curating, criticism, philosophy, sociology, anthropology, journalism, science, research, biology, ecology, economics, law, writing and translation are all welcome. WHAT IS FUNDED: fully equipped apartment with work area, optional 30 sqm visual and performing arts workshop, and a daily allowance of EUR 60 to cover meals. Travel costs to/from Andorra and mandatory medical insurance are NOT covered. DURATION: 2 to 6 weeks; stays scheduled between February and November 2027. RESIDENCY ACTIVITIES: residents must propose at least two activities to carry out during the stay (one aimed at school and/or university students); at least one activity must be executed. SELECTION: priority to projects related to Andorra and to activities most attractive to the Andorran community; diversity of backgrounds and areas of work valued; previous Faberllull Olot/Faber Andorra residents may reapply but new candidates are preferred. APPLY: fill out the Faberllull form with a 1-page cover letter and a 2-page proposal of two possible activities; documentation accepted in Catalan, Spanish, English or French. TIMELINE: deadline 13 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Tanztage Berlin Open Call 2027 (Sophiensaele)
item.description:
Organisation: Sophiensaele (Tanztage Berlin)

Location: Sophiensaele, Berlin, Germany

Award: Premieres receive EUR 10,000 to EUR 18,000 production support; revivals receive a EUR 5,000 flat fee. Both tracks also receive a EUR 410 evening fee per person per performance, rehearsal space, technical, curatorial, dramaturgical and PR support, and photo/video documentation. No application fee.

Duration: Festival 7-23 January 2027 (rehearsals from November 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

Tanztage Berlin, the long-running platform at Sophiensaele for emerging choreographers and performance makers, is accepting applications for its 2027 edition (the final edition under artistic director Mateusz Szymanowka). The festival supports work across contemporary dance, choreography, installation, video and expanded performance formats, in two tracks: premieres (new productions) and revivals (completed works). ELIGIBILITY: emerging artists connected to Berlin (early in their careers, newly living in Berlin, or without prior Berlin project funding); a meaningful professional relationship to the city is required, though formal residency registration is not. The festival particularly welcomes projects involving marginalised perspectives, intergenerational casts and disabled artists. Apply via the Tanztage Berlin open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Hubert Butler Essay Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Hubert Butler Essay Prize (with Haus Publishing)

Location: Ireland (online submission)

Award: EUR 2,500 prize (sponsored by Haus Publishing). No entry fee.

Duration: Annual essay prize

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

Annual essay prize for a previously unpublished essay of up to 3,000 words on a set theme. The 2026 theme takes Auden's line 'Poetry makes nothing happen' as a prompt about what impact high culture can have in a world facing crisis - well suited to a critical essayist working on AI, media and society. Judged blind, so it is open to and fair for debut writers (no publication history required). ELIGIBILITY: entrants must be over 18 and a citizen of the UK or an EU member state. Deadline 3 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CAIS Research Sabbaticals (Fellowships) 2027-28
item.description:
Organisation: Center for Advanced Internet Studies (CAIS), Bochum, Germany

Location: Bochum, Germany (residential at CAIS; rent-free, fully furnished apartment provided plus private office)

Award: Sabbatical leave on full salary: additional €600/month grant. No regular income: €2,000/month grant. Regular income below €1,400/month: top-up to the full grant. Alternative: reimbursement of salary or substitute costs within reasonable limits. €100/month extra per child under 18 for fellows on a full or compensatory grant. CAIS also covers one return trip to Bochum (or a daily commute if local), provides a rent-free fully furnished apartment and a private office. Fellows can request financial support for research expenses, invite a Visiting Fellow for up to 3 weeks of collaboration, and invite up to three European experts for half-day workshops (CAIS covers travel, accommodation and a daily allowance of up to €24).

Duration: 3 or 6 months. 6-month fellowships usually start in October or April; 3-month fellowships start in October, January, April or July. Exceptions possible by arrangement.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

Residential research sabbaticals at CAIS, explicitly open to excellent scholars AND practitioners across all career stages and disciplines (not academia-only). Funds individual projects on the societal impact of digital transformation, including pure research and applied projects that develop new theories, methods or perspectives for practice. Project must be self-contained with specific milestones and produce an independent output suitable for short-timeframe publication: peer-reviewed paper or conference contribution, book chapter, policy paper, or prototype. Fellows join a vibrant interdisciplinary research community with regular joint activities (breakfast Tuesdays, colloquium and dinner Wednesdays, occasional workshops Thursdays) and an international network of alumni, working groups and affiliates. In Germany, full and compensatory grants are not subject to social security contributions and are usually tax-exempt; fellows resident abroad should verify their own tax position. Note: CAIS is currently reviewing the application format and selection process for the next call, so details may change. Next call publishes at the beginning of June 2026; deadline 23 July 2026 for fellowships in the period October 2027 to March 2028. Contact: Dr. Esther Laufer, esther.laufer@cais-research.de.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Journalismfund Europe: European Cross-Border Grants
item.description:
Organisation: Journalismfund Europe

Location: Europe (cross-border projects)

Award: Variable per-team grants (typically a few thousand to ~EUR 20,000+). The July 2026 round has a total budget of around EUR 280,000.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

Supports independent investigative journalism across Europe, well-suited to surveillance, AI, platform governance and data-driven investigations. Cross-border team requirement: at least two professional journalists (freelance or staff) from at least two different countries; at least 80% of the budget must go to journalists/media from EU member states. The next round closes 30 July 2026 at 1 pm CET.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
